research_topic: Mycology with emphasis on psychedelic fungi

sources.title: Psilocybin mushroom

key_citations.title: Worldwide distribution of psilocybin mushrooms
key_citations.note: Catalogued 116 Psilocybe species, 14 Gymnopilus, 13 Panaeolus, 12 Copelandia, 6 Pluteus, 6 Inocybe, 4 Pholiotina, 1 Galerina

key_citations.authors: Pepe M, HesamidelaCerda ME, et al.

key_facts: Psilocybin mushrooms are polyphyletic and found in at least 8 genera
Mexico hosts 53 species, the highest concentration globally
Contains psilocybin (prodrug), psilocin (active), baeocystin, norbaeocystin, aeruginascin
Some species produce β-carbolines (harmine, harmane) suggesting entourage effect

sources.title: Amanita muscaria

key_citations.title: Mushrooms & Toadstools: A Study of the Activities of Fungi

key_facts: First recorded by Albertus Magnus before 1256 as fly-killing mushroom
Contains muscimol (primary psychoactive) and ibotenic acid (neurotoxic precursor)
Siberian indigenous peoples used as entheogen
Name derives from Latin 'musca' (fly) due to insecticidal use in milk
Linked to Santa Claus, berserkers, soma (speculative but culturally significant)

sources.title: Psilocybe azurescens

key_citations.title: Original species description
key_citations.note: Named by Paul Stamets and Jochen Gartz

key_facts: Most potent psilocybin mushroom documented: up to 1.8% psilocybin dry weight
Discovered in 1979 by Boy Scouts near Astoria, Oregon
Native to Pacific Northwest: Oregon coast, Columbia River Delta, Washington
Causes 'wood lover's paralysis' — temporary motor weakness of unknown etiology
Fruiting season: late September through early January

sources.title: Foxfire

key_citations.note: First recorded documentation of bioluminescent fungi

key_citations.title: Bioluminescence: the First 3000 Years (Review)
key_citations.journal: Journal of Siberian Federal University. Biology

key_facts: Bioluminescence from luciferin-luciferase reaction in decaying wood fungi
Species include Panellus stipticus, Omphalotus olearius, Omphalotus nidiformis
Used to illuminate American Turtle submarine compass and barometer
Aristotle noted it was 'cold to the touch' unlike fire
May function to attract spore-dispersing insects or warn predators

sources.title: Panaeolus cyanescens

key_citations.note: Reported ~2.5% psilocybin, ~1.194% psilocin — highest potency in published research

key_citations.title: Mushrooms Demystified: A Comprehensive Guide to the Fleshy Fungi

key_facts: Highest psilocybin concentration in academic literature: ~2.5%
Common names: Blue Meanies, Pan Cyan, Hawaiian Copelandia
Coprophilous (dung-loving) species of tropical/subtropical distribution
Found across Neotropics and Paleotropics: Africa, SE Asia, Americas, Oceania
Also contains serotonin, urea, and baeocystin

sources.title: Ergot
key_facts: Claviceps genus contains ~50 species, mostly tropical
C. purpurea produces ergotamine, ergine (LSA), and lysergic acid derivatives
Ergotism (St. Anthony's Fire) caused mass poisonings in medieval Europe
2020 amber fossil established minimum age for psychotropic compounds in fungi
Source material for Albert Hofmann's synthesis of LSD-25 in 1938

sources.title: Psilocybe semilanceata
key_facts: Common name 'Liberty Cap' from resemblance to Phrygian cap
First European psilocybin intoxication recorded: 1799 in London's Green Park
First European species confirmed to contain psilocybin (1960s)
Widely distributed in temperate Northern Hemisphere
Saprotrophic on decaying grass roots (not coprophilous)

sources.title: Psilocybe cubensis

key_citations.title: Original description from Cuba as Stropharia cubensis

key_facts: Most widely cultivated psilocybin mushroom due to ease of indoor growth
Common names: Golden Teacher, Gold Cap, San Ysidro, Palenque mushroom
Thai name 'Hed keequai' = 'mushroom after water buffalo defecates'
Moderate potency, wide tropical distribution
Previously classified as Stropharia cubensis

additional_reading.author: Stamets P
additional_reading.title: Psilocybin Mushrooms of the World
additional_reading.note: Definitive field guide by renowned mycologist

additional_reading.author: Wasson RG
additional_reading.title: Soma: Divine Mushroom of Immortality
additional_reading.note: Controversial but influential work on Amanita muscaria and ancient religions

additional_reading.author: Hofmann A
additional_reading.title: LSD: My Problem Child
additional_reading.note: Autobiography by the discoverer of LSD, with discussion of ergot

additional_reading.author: McKenna T
additional_reading.title: Food of the Gods: The Search for the Original Tree of Knowledge
additional_reading.note: Speculative but engaging theory of psychedelic co-evolution
